title: bd show: resolve bare suffix code when unambiguous
close_reason: ResolveID now matches bare suffix codes (prefix-of-suffix) when unambiguous; added resolve_test.go

title: Dependency direction display: add tests for bd show wording; fix dep tree traversing dependents instead of dependencies
close_reason: bd show wording covered by tests; dep tree now follows dependencies (epic shows children) and renders nesting correctly (was flat); cycle-safe

title: store.Save() drops unknown JSONL fields on round-trip (design, notes) against full-beads files
description:
internal/store/store.go Save()/SaveToFile() re-encode every issue from types.Issue, so any JSON key absent from that struct is silently destroyed on the next write. Load()/loadFromFile() discards them at unmarshal time.

bd-lite's types.Issue knows 14 keys. Upstream beads writes more (design, acceptance_criteria, notes, estimated_minutes, external_ref, source_repo, content_hash, created_by).

Blast radius measured against ~/projects/beads-tui/.beads/issues.jsonl (164 issues, tracked by real beads until Feb 2026, now on bd-lite via PATH):
  design      6 issues  (tui-qxy.1/.2/.4/.5/.7, tui-tjf -- tui-tjf holds a full theme-system architecture doc)
  notes       5 issues  (tui-1oj holds a multi-paragraph root-cause writeup)
  created_by  1 issue   (tui-6f1)

Any single bd create/update/close/comment/dep-add in that repo rewrites all 164 lines and drops all of the above. Recoverable from git (issues.jsonl is tracked) but entirely silent.

This blocks filing the beads-tui sister ticket for created_by, since doing so with the bd on PATH would trigger the loss.

Fix options:
  a) widen types.Issue with every upstream field -- brittle, upstream keeps adding
  b) preserve unknowns generically: Extra map[string]json.RawMessage plus custom MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON -- robust, keeps bd-lite a lossless citizen of the beads wire format
  c) document bd-lite as lossy against full-beads files and refuse to write when unknown keys are present
close_reason: types.Issue now round-trips unknown JSONL keys via Extra map; marshalNoEscape prevents HTML re-escaping of design/notes

title: Record created_by on issue create via new internal/actor; unify cmd/comment.go author
description:
Add CreatedBy string `json:"created_by,omitempty"` to types.Issue, after Assignee. Wire-compatible with upstream beads (beads/internal/types/types.go:40).

New package internal/actor with Name(): $BD_ACTOR -> git config user.name -> $USER -> "" (field omitted). gitUserName is a package-level var func so tests stub it without a temp git repo. Trimmed from upstream's chain: no --actor flag, no $BEADS_ACTOR, no literal "unknown" sentinel.

Call Name() lazily at the two write sites. Do NOT hoist into rootCmd.PersistentPreRunE, which would fork a git subprocess on every bd list/show/ready.

cmd/create.go: set CreatedBy: actor.Name().
cmd/comment.go: drop os/user import, pass actor.Name() to st.AddComment. Behavior change: new comments record the git display name where they used to record the OS username. Existing comments render unchanged.
Dependency.CreatedBy (types.go:93) stays dead; nothing displays it.
bd update / bd close do not touch created_by.

Display: suffix the existing Created line in output.go PrintIssue, omitting the suffix when absent so legacy issues render byte-identically.
  Created:  2026-07-09 12:07 by Andy Nutter-Upham
  Created:  2026-06-30 17:45

Tests: internal/actor/actor_test.go covers the four resolution branches with t.Setenv + stubbed gitUserName. output_test.go asserts the by-suffix appears with CreatedBy set and the line is unchanged without it.

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-09-whodunnit-design.md

title: Show age column in bd list/ready; extend output.Age() past days and split out formatAge
description:
internal/output/output.go:251 defines Age() and nothing calls it. Wire it into PrintIssueList as a right-aligned 4-wide column between type and title:

  fmt.Printf("%s %s  P%d  %-12s %4s  %s\n", status, issue.ID, issue.Priority, issue.IssueType, Age(issue.CreatedAt), issue.Title)

  [ ] bd-lite-x1y  P2  feature        2h  Record whodunnit on beads
  [>] bd-lite-c3d  P1  bug           12d  Fix dep tree direction
  [ ] bd-lite-z8w  P4  chore          2y  Drop the vendored beads checkout

12mo is the widest emittable value, so 4 is the right width. store.go:93 already sorts by CreatedAt, so the column reads monotonically down the list. bd ready shares the printer and gets it too.

Age() bottoms out at days, so a year-old backlog item renders '731d'. Extend through mo/y. Split the pure formatting out of the clock read so it is testable against fixed durations, and clamp negative durations (clock skew, hand-edited future timestamps) to zero:

  func Age(t time.Time) string { return formatAge(time.Since(t)) }
  func formatAge(d time.Duration) string  // m -> h -> d -> mo -> y

Boundaries: <1h m, <24h h, <90d d, <365d mo, else y.

This changes default bd list output for anything parsing it. Accepted.

Tests: table test over formatAge with fixed durations incl. negative and each boundary; PrintIssueList asserts the column renders.

Independent of bd-lite-gh8 but edits the same file (different function); land sequentially, not in parallel worktrees.

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-09-whodunnit-design.md
